name: Mazz
description:
'''Mazz''' ({{IPAc-en|'|m|ɑː|z}} {{respell|MAH-z}}) was an American [[Tejano music|Tejano]] band formed in [[Brownsville, Texas]] in 1977.{{Sfn|San Miguel|2002|p=106-108}} The band was formed by its foremost members, singer [[Joe López]] and guitarist and arranger [[Jimmy González]]. The band became known for their unique and innovative style of [[rancheras]] and [[Mexican cumbia|cumbia]]. After signing with and moving to the then-upcoming record label [[Capitol/EMI Latin]] in 1989, they would become one of the Tejano genre's most popular bands during its 1990s [[golden age]].

Mazz disbanded in 1998, with frontmen López and González going on to form, respectively, [[Joe López y la Nueva Imagen Mazz]] in 2001 and [[Jimmy González y Grupo Mazz]] in 1999.

In 2006, López was convicted of and imprisoned for sexually assaulting his niece. After his release in 2018, he formed another group, [[Joe López#Joe López y Grupo Mazz|Joe López y Grupo Mazz]] and continues to write, record, and perform.
